revision.text:
{{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|25.7425|N|123.4714|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the [[Senkaku Islands]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the [[East China Sea]]. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the [[Sumiyoshi-kai]], and wooden ''[[hokora]]'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the [[End of World War II in Asia|end of World War II]] that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.
== References == {{reflist}}
[[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Senkaku Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Senkaku jinja}} [[Category:Religious organizations established in 2000]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Okinawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Religious buildings and structures completed in 2000]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[en:Senkaku Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[en:Senkaku Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[en:Senkaku Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[en:Senkaku Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:refreshing the bump]][[Category:misc bump Category]][[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Senkaku Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]][[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[Category:bump February 14, 2025 bump]] {{Shinmei shrines}} [[Category:New]]

Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.

{{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|25.7425|N|123.4714|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the [[Senkaku Islands]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the [[East China Sea]]. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the [[Sumiyoshi-kai]], and wooden ''[[hokora]]'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the [[End of World War II in Asia|end of World War II]] that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.

{{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|25.7425|N|123.4714|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the [[Senkaku Islands]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the [[East China Sea]]. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the [[Sumiyoshi-kai]], and wooden ''[[hokora]]'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the [[End of World War II in Asia|end of World War II]] that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.

{{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image = | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|25.7425|N|123.4714|E|source:wikidata|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Amaterasu | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the [[Senkaku Islands]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the [[East China Sea]]. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the [[Sumiyoshi-kai]], and wooden ''[[hokora]]'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the [[End of World War II in Asia|end of World War II]] that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q391408]] Wikidata name: Senkaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image =File:Ishigaki senkaku jinja.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|25.7425|N|123.47138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ishigaki|WD=Q838366}} }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the [[:en:Senkaku Islands|Senkaku Islands]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the [[:en:East China Sea|East China Sea]]. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the [[:en:Sumiyoshi-kai|Sumiyoshi-kai]], and wooden ''[[:en:Hokora|Hokora]]'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the [[:en:End of World War II in Asia|end of World War II]] that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.
== References == {{reflist}} ]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q391408]] Wikidata name: Senkaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image =File:Ishigaki senkaku jinja.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|25.7425|N|123.47138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ishigaki|WD=Q838366}} }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the [[:en:Senkaku Islands|Senkaku Islands]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the [[:en:East China Sea|East China Sea]]. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the [[:en:Sumiyoshi-kai|Sumiyoshi-kai]], and wooden ''[[:en:Hokora|Hokora]]'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the [[:en:End of World War II in Asia|end of World War II]] that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q391408]] Wikidata name: Senkaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image =File:Ishigaki senkaku jinja.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|25.7425|N|123.47138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ishigaki|WD=Q838366}} }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the [[:en:Senkaku Islands|Senkaku Islands]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the [[:en:East China Sea|East China Sea]]. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the [[:en:Sumiyoshi-kai|Sumiyoshi-kai]], and wooden ''[[:en:Hokora|Hokora]]'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the [[:en:End of World War II in Asia|end of World War II]] that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the [[:en:Japan Coast Guard|Japan Coast Guard]].
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.
== References == {{reflist}} ]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q391408]] Wikidata name: Senkaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image =File:Ishigaki senkaku jinja.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|25.7425|N|123.47138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258}} | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ishigaki|WD=Q838366}} }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the {{ill|Senkaku Islands|en|Senkaku Islands|WD=Q29460|lt=Senkaku Islands}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the {{ill|East China Sea|en|East China Sea|WD=Q45341|lt=East China Sea}}. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-kai|en|Sumiyoshi-kai|WD=Q2349753|lt=Sumiyoshi-kai}}, and wooden ''{{ill|Hokora|en|Hokora|WD=Q1442984|lt=Hokora}}'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the {{ill|End of World War II in Asia|en|End of World War II in Asia|WD=Q5375854|lt=end of World War II}} that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|WD=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard}}.
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Senkaku Shrine]][[fr:Senkaku-jinja]][[ja:尖閣神社]][[ko:센카쿠 신사]][[zh:尖閣神社]]
{{short description|Shinto shrine in Okinawa Prefecture, Japan}} {{Refimprove|date=October 2010}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Senkaku Jinja | image =File:Ishigaki senkaku jinja.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|25.7425|N|123.47138889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|1=Yamato Takeru}} | founded_by = | established = 2000 | date_destroyed = | location = Uotsuri-jima, Senkaku Islands | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Ishigaki|WD=Q838366|1=Ishigaki, Okinawa}} }} {{nihongo|'''Senkaku Jinja'''|尖閣神社}} is a Shinto shrine located on Uotsuri-jima in the {{ill|Senkaku Islands|en|Senkaku Islands|WD=Q29460|lt=Senkaku Islands|1=Senkaku Islands}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is dedicated to [[Amaterasu]].
The shrine was founded on April 20, 2000 and serves to pray for the safe passage of all boats through the Senkaku Islands and the {{ill|East China Sea|en|East China Sea|WD=Q45341|lt=East China Sea|1=East China Sea}}. Construction was led by [[Nihon Seinensha]], a right-wing organization affiliated with the {{ill|Sumiyoshi-kai|en|Sumiyoshi-kai|WD=Q2349753|lt=Sumiyoshi-kai|1=Sumiyoshi-kai}}, and wooden ''{{ill|Hokora|en|Hokora|WD=Q1442984|lt=Hokora|1=Hokora}}'' were also constructed. When the shrine was founded, it was the first time since the {{ill|End of World War II in Asia|en|End of World War II in Asia|WD=Q5375854|lt=end of World War II|1=End of World War II in Asia}} that a ''[[kannushi]]'' had been on the island. Every year, the two lighthouses built by Seinensha on the island are inspected and serviced, and during this time a yearly festival occurs, and prayers are issued for safe passage. In 2006, Seinensha donated the lighthouses to the government and they are now a national property administered by the {{ill|Japan Coast Guard|en|Japan Coast Guard|WD=Q565418|lt=Japan Coast Guard|1=Japan Coast Guard}}.
In March 2004, several Chinese activists illegally landed on the island. Seinensha reported the incident to the [[Okinawa Police]], who arrested the activists and made a damage report.<ref>{{cite news|title=Japan, China out of step in handling of fishing boat collision incident |url=http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |accessdate=15 October 2010 |newspaper=Mainichi Daily News |date=21 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20100923170330/http://mdn.mainichi.jp/perspectives/news/20100921p2a00m0na002000c.html |archivedate=September 23, 2010 }}</ref> Afterwards, Seinensha rebuilt the ''hokora'' and erected a plinth with the words "Senkaku Jinja" written on it. The ''hokora'' and plinth are built of solid granite.
